definition: Anthropic was founded in 2021 by researchers—including Dario and Daniela Amodei—who left OpenAI to build an AI company centered on safety, scaling research, and the Claude assistant line that followed.
layman: Anthropic started as a new AI lab in 2021 by people who wanted to focus heavily on making advanced AI safer, then grew into the company behind Claude.
content:
## History & milestones

Anthropic's history is tightly coupled with the **modern LLM era**: rapid scaling, public chat products, and intensifying debate over AI risk. This topic gives a **high-level timeline**—not every model release date. Verify current product names in Anthropic announcements when precision matters.

### Before Anthropic (context)

Several founders previously worked on large-scale language model research elsewhere in the industry, gaining experience with **scaling laws**, **alignment challenges**, and **deployment lessons** that informed Anthropic's safety-first framing from day one.

### 2021 — Founding

- **Anthropic PBC founded** with explicit emphasis on **AI safety** and beneficial outcomes.
- Early work focused on research directions (alignment, interpretability, scalable oversight) rather than immediate mass-market chat.

### 2022–2023 — Research visibility & Claude emergence

- Influential papers and posts (e.g., **Constitutional AI**, core views on AI safety) articulated Anthropic's technical agenda.
- **Claude** models became available to select partners and developers via **API access**, expanding who could experiment with Anthropic's stack.
- **claude.ai** brought a consumer chat interface to a broader audience, parallel to API growth.

### 2023 onward — Model tiers & ecosystem expansion

- The **Opus / Sonnet / Haiku** naming scheme communicated capability and cost tiers across releases (exact generations evolve—check docs for current IDs).
- Partnerships with **cloud platforms** (e.g., Bedrock, Vertex, Foundry paths documented in later roadmap stages) broadened enterprise access patterns.
- Developer offerings expanded—**Console**, SDKs, **Claude Code**, and later agent-oriented APIs—moving Claude from "chat model" to **platform**.

### How to read this timeline as a learner

| Era | What changed for you |
| --- | --- |
| Research-heavy early | Safety concepts (Constitutional AI) shaped training philosophy |
| API era | You integrate Claude into apps programmatically |
| Chat + team products | Non-developers adopt Claude daily via claude.ai and apps |
| Platform era | Tools, agents, and enterprise deployment patterns multiply |

Detailed research citations sit in **Research & Publications**; mission framing in **Mission & Values**.
keyPoints: Anthropic founded in 2021 with safety-centric AI lab DNA.
Constitutional AI and safety essays preceded wide Claude adoption.
Claude reached developers via API and consumers via claude.ai in the 2022–2023 window.
Model tiers (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) and cloud partnerships expanded enterprise access.
Exact release dates and model IDs change—confirm in official announcements.
